question: WHY DOES MY BALL REV MORE WHEN I PLAY 2 HANDS AND LESS WHEN I USE MY THUMB?
content: WHEN YOU DONT USE THE THUMB, YOU DONT HAVE TO WORRY ABOUT ALL THE ISSUES OF A LATE THUMB SUCH AS: When you use your thumb, a quick and clean release of your thumb is the goal. Your thumb must exit the ball slightly before your fingers rotate the ball. If you squeeze your thumb too tightly, it can prevent your thumb from exiting quickly and cleanly, which affects your ball's skid distance. You should use very light gripping pressure with your thumb and keep it straight, without a knuckle-bend. This helps you avoid hanging up in the thumb hole and ensures a consistent release point. Keeping firm gripping pressure on your finger pads, not your thumb, allows you to rotate the ball consistently at the same moment of release. After your thumb leaves the ball, you should rely on only a slight finger rotation, perhaps an inch, without rotating your arm
